---
title: "Deployment Drift Monitoring"
jupyter: venv_ml
---

# Deployment Drift Monitoring

This chapter evaluates model stability across simulated production traffic. The
holdout set (13,917 encounters) is divided into four temporal batches representing
semi-annual scoring periods (~6 months each, covering approximately 2007–2008),
fed through the deployed model in encounter order.
All metrics use the **training set as the reference distribution** — the same
baseline the model was built on.

## Setup

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
import json
import pathlib
import warnings

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker
import joblib
from scipy import stats
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

repo_root = pathlib.Path().resolve().parents[1]

# Load pre-computed drift report (generated by monitoring/drift.py)
DRIFT_PATH = repo_root / "monitoring" / "drift_report.json"
drift_results = json.loads(DRIFT_PATH.read_text())
df_drift = pd.DataFrame(drift_results)

TAU = 0.12
N_BATCHES = len(df_drift)
```

## Batch Summary

The holdout set is ordered by encounter index (reflecting the temporal encounter_id
sort from the Snowflake mart) and divided into four equal-sized semi-annual batches
(~3,479 encounters each, representing ~6-month windows across 2007–2008).

```{python}
#| label: batch-summary
#| code-fold: true
summary = df_drift[[
    "batch", "n_rows", "auc_roc", "flag_rate", "pos_rate",
    "ks_stat", "ks_pval", "mean_psi", "drift_alert"
]].copy()
summary.columns = [
    "Batch", "N", "AUC-ROC", "Flag Rate", "Pos Rate",
    "KS Stat", "KS p-val", "Mean PSI", "Alert"
]
summary["Batch"] = summary["Batch"].apply(lambda x: f"P{x}")
summary["Alert"] = summary["Alert"].map({True: "⚠", False: "✓"})
summary.set_index("Batch", inplace=True)
summary.style \
    .format({
        "AUC-ROC":   "{:.4f}",
        "Flag Rate": "{:.3f}",
        "Pos Rate":  "{:.3f}",
        "KS Stat":   "{:.4f}",
        "KS p-val":  "{:.4f}",
        "Mean PSI":  "{:.4f}",
    }) \
    .background_gradient(subset=["AUC-ROC"], cmap="RdYlGn", vmin=0.60, vmax=0.75) \
    .background_gradient(subset=["Mean PSI"], cmap="YlOrRd", vmin=0, vmax=0.25)
```

**Reference distribution**: training set (48,706 encounters).
**Alert thresholds**: Mean PSI > 0.20 or KS statistic > 0.10.

---

## AUC-ROC and Flag Rate Over Time

```{python}
#| label: auc-flagrate-plot
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "AUC-ROC and flag rate across temporal batches. Dashed lines show the validation reference."
fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(10, 4))

quarters = [f"P{r['batch']}" for r in drift_results]
aucs      = [r["auc_roc"]   for r in drift_results]
flagrates = [r["flag_rate"] for r in drift_results]
posrates  = [r["pos_rate"]  for r in drift_results]

# AUC-ROC
ax1.plot(quarters, aucs, marker="o", color="#2196F3", linewidth=2, markersize=7, label="Holdout batch")
ax1.axhline(0.6866, color="#9E9E9E", linestyle="--", linewidth=1.2, label="Validation AUC")
ax1.set_title("AUC-ROC by Period (~6 mo)", fontweight="bold")
ax1.set_ylabel("AUC-ROC")
ax1.set_ylim(0.60, 0.75)
ax1.legend(fontsize=9)
ax1.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.4)
for i, (q, v) in enumerate(zip(quarters, aucs)):
    ax1.annotate(f"{v:.3f}", (q, v), textcoords="offset points",
                 xytext=(0, 8), ha="center", fontsize=8)

# Flag rate and positive rate
ax2.plot(quarters, flagrates, marker="s", color="#FF5722", linewidth=2,
         markersize=7, label="Flag rate")
ax2.plot(quarters, posrates,  marker="^", color="#4CAF50", linewidth=2,
         markersize=7, label="Positive rate")
ax2.axhline(TAU * 0.9 + 0.02 * 0, 0, 1, color="white")   # padding
ax2.axhline(0.157, color="#9E9E9E", linestyle="--", linewidth=1.2,
            label=f"Part 1 target (15.7%)")
ax2.set_title("Flag Rate vs Positive Rate", fontweight="bold")
ax2.set_ylabel("Rate")
ax2.set_ylim(0, 0.25)
ax2.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.PercentFormatter(xmax=1))
ax2.legend(fontsize=9)
ax2.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.4)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## KS Statistic — Score Distribution Drift

The KS statistic measures whether the predicted probability distribution in each
batch has shifted relative to the training reference.

```{python}
#| label: ks-plot
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "KS statistic per batch (vs. training). Dashed line = alert threshold (0.10)."

# Recompute score distributions locally for the CDF plot
DATA_DIR = repo_root / "ml" / "data"
TARGET   = "readmitted_30day"

model        = joblib.load(DATA_DIR / "xgb_model.joblib")
preprocessor = joblib.load(DATA_DIR / "preprocessor.joblib")

def _sanitize(name):
    return name.replace("[", "(").replace("]", ")").replace("<", "lt")

_DROP = {
    "encounter_id", "patient_nbr", "readmitted_raw", "readmitted_any",
    "split_group", "admission_type_id", "discharge_disposition_id",
    "admission_source_id", "readmitted_30day",
    "glimepiride_pioglitazone", "metformin_pioglitazone",
    "metformin_rosiglitazone", "glipizide_metformin", "tolbutamide",
    "miglitol", "tolazamide", "chlorpropamide", "acarbose",
    "glyburide_metformin", "nateglinide",
}

def _predict_raw(df_raw):
    X = df_raw.drop(columns=[c for c in _DROP if c in df_raw.columns])
    X_arr  = preprocessor.transform(X)
    names  = [_sanitize(c) for c in preprocessor.get_feature_names_out()]
    X_df   = pd.DataFrame(X_arr, columns=names)
    return model.predict_proba(X_df)[:, 1]

raw     = pd.read_parquet(DATA_DIR / "raw_features.parquet")
train_r = raw[raw["split_group"] == "train"]
holdout = raw[raw["split_group"] == "holdout"].reset_index(drop=True)

batch_size = len(holdout) // N_BATCHES
batches = [
    holdout.iloc[i * batch_size : (i + 1) * batch_size if i < N_BATCHES - 1 else len(holdout)]
    for i in range(N_BATCHES)
]

train_probs  = _predict_raw(train_r)
batch_probs  = [_predict_raw(b) for b in batches]
ks_stats     = [stats.ks_2samp(train_probs, bp).statistic for bp in batch_probs]

fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 4))

# KS bar chart
colors = ["#F44336" if v > 0.10 else "#2196F3" for v in ks_stats]
ax1.bar(quarters, ks_stats, color=colors, alpha=0.85, edgecolor="white")
ax1.axhline(0.10, color="#F44336", linestyle="--", linewidth=1.5,
            label="Alert threshold (0.10)")
ax1.set_title("KS Statistic vs Training", fontweight="bold")
ax1.set_ylabel("KS Statistic")
ax1.set_ylim(0, 0.15)
ax1.legend(fontsize=9)
ax1.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.4)
for q, v in zip(quarters, ks_stats):
    ax1.text(q, v + 0.001, f"{v:.4f}", ha="center", fontsize=8)

# CDF overlay
prob_range = np.linspace(0, 0.5, 500)
train_cdf  = np.array([(train_probs <= p).mean() for p in prob_range])
ax2.plot(prob_range, train_cdf, color="black", linewidth=2, label="Train", zorder=5)
palette = ["#2196F3", "#4CAF50", "#FF9800", "#9C27B0"]
for j, (bp, q) in enumerate(zip(batch_probs, quarters)):
    cdf = np.array([(bp <= p).mean() for p in prob_range])
    ax2.plot(prob_range, cdf, color=palette[j], linewidth=1.5,
             linestyle="--", alpha=0.9, label=q)
ax2.axvline(TAU, color="#F44336", linestyle=":", linewidth=1.2, label=f"τ={TAU}")
ax2.set_title("Score CDF: Training vs Batches", fontweight="bold")
ax2.set_xlabel("Predicted Probability")
ax2.set_ylabel("Cumulative Proportion")
ax2.legend(fontsize=9)
ax2.grid(alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## PSI — Input Feature Drift

PSI is computed on key input features (top SHAP contributors) against the
training distribution. PSI < 0.10 indicates no significant shift; 0.10–0.20
moderate; > 0.20 triggers an alert.

The shift in `number_diagnoses` between 1999-2005 (training) and 2007-2008 (holdout) is clinically plausible and historically documented. This period saw significant expansion of ICD-9 coding practices in US hospitals — hospitals increasingly coded more diagnoses per encounter driven by reimbursement incentives under DRG-based payment systems. The observed distributional shift likely reflects coding practice evolution rather than genuine changes in patient comorbidity burden. The coding practice drift in `number_diagnoses` may continue or accelerate beyond the 2008 cutoff of this dataset. In a real deployment this would warrant a retraining trigger note — not because current performance is degraded, but because the feature distribution is on a trajectory that could eventually matter if `number_diagnoses` weight increases with new data.

The decoupling of feature-level drift (`number_diagnoses` PSI=0.318) from prediction-level stability (flag rate and AUC within normal range across all batches) demonstrates that the model's predictive signal is concentrated in stable, high-weight features. This is a desirable property for production deployment.

However, perhaps PSI alerts should be weighted by feature importance. A feature with SHAP rank #5 triggering a monitoring alert while features ranked #1-4 are stable is a low-priority signal. The monitoring framework should distinguish between drift in consequential features vs drift in peripheral features.



```{python}
#| label: psi-heatmap
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "PSI per feature and batch. Red = moderate drift (0.10–0.20). Orange dashed = alert threshold (0.20)."

psi_rows = []
for r in drift_results:
    row = {"Batch": f"P{r['batch']}"}
    row.update(r["psi_by_feature"])
    psi_rows.append(row)

df_psi = pd.DataFrame(psi_rows).set_index("Batch")

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4),
                         gridspec_kw={"width_ratios": [1, 2]})

# Heatmap
import matplotlib.colors as mcolors
norm  = mcolors.Normalize(vmin=0, vmax=0.25)
cmap  = plt.get_cmap("YlOrRd")
im    = axes[0].imshow(df_psi.values, aspect="auto", cmap=cmap, norm=norm)
axes[0].set_xticks(range(len(df_psi.columns)))
axes[0].set_xticklabels(df_psi.columns, rotation=45, ha="right", fontsize=8)
axes[0].set_yticks(range(len(df_psi.index)))
axes[0].set_yticklabels(df_psi.index)
axes[0].set_title("PSI by Feature & Period", fontweight="bold")
for ri in range(df_psi.shape[0]):
    for ci in range(df_psi.shape[1]):
        v = df_psi.values[ri, ci]
        axes[0].text(ci, ri, f"{v:.3f}", ha="center", va="center",
                     fontsize=7.5, color="black" if v < 0.15 else "white")
plt.colorbar(im, ax=axes[0], label="PSI")

# Mean PSI line
mean_psi = df_drift["mean_psi"].values
axes[1].plot(quarters, mean_psi, marker="o", color="#FF5722", linewidth=2,
             markersize=8, label="Mean PSI")
axes[1].fill_between(quarters, mean_psi, alpha=0.15, color="#FF5722")
axes[1].axhline(0.10, color="#FF9800", linestyle="--", linewidth=1.2,
                label="Moderate threshold (0.10)")
axes[1].axhline(0.20, color="#F44336", linestyle="--", linewidth=1.5,
                label="Alert threshold (0.20)")
axes[1].set_title("Mean PSI Over Time", fontweight="bold")
axes[1].set_ylabel("Mean PSI")
axes[1].set_ylim(0, 0.30)
axes[1].legend(fontsize=9)
axes[1].grid(axis="y", alpha=0.4)
for q, v in zip(quarters, mean_psi):
    axes[1].annotate(f"{v:.4f}", (q, v), textcoords="offset points",
                     xytext=(0, 8), ha="center", fontsize=8)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## Interpretation

```{python}
#| label: drift-narrative
#| echo: false

max_ks   = df_drift["ks_stat"].max()
max_psi  = df_drift["mean_psi"].max()
n_alerts = df_drift["drift_alert"].sum()
auc_q1   = df_drift.loc[0, "auc_roc"]
auc_q4   = df_drift.loc[N_BATCHES - 1, "auc_roc"]
auc_drop = auc_q1 - auc_q4

print(f"Batches evaluated : {N_BATCHES}  (τ = {TAU})")
print(f"Drift alerts      : {n_alerts} / {N_BATCHES}")
print(f"Max KS statistic  : {max_ks:.4f}  (threshold 0.10)")
print(f"Max mean PSI      : {max_psi:.4f}  (threshold 0.20)")
print(f"AUC-ROC P1 → P4  : {auc_q1:.4f} → {auc_q4:.4f}  (Δ {auc_drop:+.4f})")
```

**No drift alerts triggered** across all four simulated semi-annual periods. Key findings:

- **Score distribution (KS)**: All batches remain well below the 0.10 alert threshold.
  KS statistics trend slightly upward from P1 to P3 before stabilising in P4,
  reflecting the expected score distribution shift as holdout encounters are
  temporally later than the training period.

- **Input features (PSI)**: Mean PSI ranges 0.04–0.06 across periods — firmly
  in the "no significant change" range (< 0.10). The gradual increase from P1
  to P4 is consistent with the temporal split: later encounters naturally diverge
  slightly from the 1999–2004 training distribution.

- **AUC-ROC**: P1 achieves the highest discrimination (0.699), declining modestly
  through P2–P3 before partially recovering in P4. All values remain within
  ±0.04 of the validation reference (0.687), indicating that predictive
  performance is stable across the deployment window.

- **Flag rate**: Ranges 13.9%–15.0% across periods, closely tracking the Part 1
  design target of 15.7%. The slight upward trend in P4 is matched by a higher
  positive rate (6.8% vs ~5% in P1–P3), suggesting the model self-corrects to
  the elevated event frequency rather than blindly over-flagging.

**Production recommendation**: No retraining indicated based on these simulated
semi-annual batches. Monitoring cadence should be maintained; an alert would
be triggered if KS > 0.10 or mean PSI > 0.20 in any future scoring window.
